steps.title: Welcome to Day 5
steps.body: Big goals can feel overwhelming. Long to-do lists can be paralyzing. Today you will learn how to use AI to break any goal into a clear, realistic plan — and then actually use it.

steps.title: AI is a great planning partner
steps.body: AI does not get overwhelmed. You give it a goal, constraints, and any context. It produces a structured plan in seconds. You can then edit, adjust, and make it your own.

steps.title: Sound familiar?
steps.body: You have a goal that feels big: start a side project, get fit, learn something new, reorganize your home. You know what you want — but you have no idea where to begin. AI can give you a first map.

steps.title: The three planning questions
steps.body: Before you ask AI to plan anything, know: 1. What is the goal? 2. What are the constraints (time, money, energy)? 3. How specific do you want the output — high-level overview or a step-by-step breakdown?

steps.title: A goal breakdown prompt
steps.prompt: Act as a personal productivity coach. My goal is to start a small online store selling handmade candles within 60 days. I have about five hours a week to work on this and a budget of $300. Break this goal into a realistic weekly action plan for the first four weeks. Keep each week to three clear tasks.

steps.title: What made that prompt work
steps.body: It included a role (productivity coach), the goal (online store), time available (5 hours/week), budget ($300), timeline (60 days), and output format (weekly tasks). Nothing was left for AI to guess.

steps.title: Quick check
steps.question: You want AI to help you plan a kitchen renovation. Which prompt will get the best result?
options: How do I renovate a kitchen?

Act as a home renovation planner. I want to renovate my kitchen in the next three months with a budget of $5,000. I can work on it on weekends only. Give me a four-phase plan with the most important tasks in each phase.
What are some kitchen renovation tips?
feedback: Possible, but too vague — AI will produce a generic answer.
This will give you general information, not a plan tailored to your situation.
Excellent. Role, goal, constraints, timeline, and output format — all included.
Tips are useful but not the same as a personalized action plan.

steps.title: Two approaches
steps.question: Try this: ask AI to plan your goal two ways — once as a high-level three-step overview, and once as a detailed week-by-week plan. Compare them. Which format is more useful for how you actually think and work?

steps.title: True or false?
steps.question: Once AI gives you a plan, you should follow it exactly without making any changes.
feedback: The AI plan is a starting point, not a final answer. Edit it, adjust it, and make it fit your real life.
Right. Think of the AI plan as a strong first draft — yours to improve from there.

pairs.left: I have 20 tasks and no idea what to do first
pairs.right: Help me prioritize these by impact and urgency

pairs.left: My goal feels too big to start
pairs.right: Break this goal into the first three steps

pairs.left: I keep forgetting to do important things
pairs.right: Turn this list into a simple weekly schedule

steps.title: Good fit?
steps.question: Help me prioritize my tasks for this week. Here they are: [list of 10 tasks]. I have about six hours free and need to finish the client report first.
feedback: Yes — giving AI your task list with your constraints is a practical and powerful use.
Actually this is a strong planning prompt. Context plus a concrete list works well.

steps.title: Build a goal breakdown prompt

fields.label: What goal do you want to plan?
fields.placeholder: e.g. learn to speak basic Spanish

fields.label: How much time do you have, and over what period?
fields.placeholder: e.g. 20 minutes a day for 8 weeks

fields.label: How do you want the plan formatted?
fields.placeholder: e.g. a weekly plan with three tasks per week

steps.template: Act as a personal productivity coach. My goal is to {{goal}}. I have {{timeframe}} to work on this. Give me {{output}}.

steps.title: Copy your planning prompt
steps.body: Copy your prompt and paste it into your AI tool to see the plan it creates.

steps.title: Paste your plan
steps.body: Paste the plan AI gave you here. We will save it so you can refer back to it.

questions.label: How realistic did the plan feel for your actual life?
options: Very realistic — I could start tomorrow
Too ambitious — I need to scale it down
Too general — I need more detail

questions.label: What is one thing you would change or add to make the plan work better for you?

questions.question: What three things should every planning prompt include?
options: The goal, your constraints, and the output format you want
The topic, a long explanation, and a thank-you

questions.question: AI-generated plans should always be treated as a starting point that you adapt.
When a goal feels too big to start, ask AI to break it into the first ______ steps.

steps.title: Day 5 complete
steps.body: You now know how to turn any overwhelming goal into a clear, structured plan — with AI as your thinking partner. The next time something feels too big to start, you have a process. See you tomorrow.
